repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
When Cython analyses type annotation containing `cython.XYZ[...]` (`cython.const[]`, `cython.pointer[]`) it tries to look up Cython scope to find type matching `XYZ` name. 

https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/62a4106657b31485aed27ecdc42c68012b02cd8c/Cython/Compiler/ExprNodes.py#L4188
https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/62a4106657b31485aed27ecdc42c68012b02cd8c/Cython/Compiler/ExprNodes.py#L7962

There is no such type (`const`, `pointer`) hence Cython initializes CythonScope which ends up loading memoryview utility code.

https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/62a4106657b31485aed27ecdc42c68012b02cd8c/Cython/Compiler/CythonScope.py#L55
https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/62a4106657b31485aed27ecdc42c68012b02cd8c/Cython/Compiler/CythonScope.py#L136

This PR simply detects Cython scope valid attributes which occurs in type definition but are not type itself and just returns expected `None` without loading Cython scope.

Closes #7487

comment_data.body:
This is definitely missing `volatile`.

I'm a bit suspicious of this solution in general because I think the list is incomplete (and I don't think `volatile` makes it complete).

I think it'd be better to be more selective about loading the `view` submodule only when `view` is specifically looked. I don't know how hard that is though.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@
 from . import MemoryView
 from .StringEncoding import EncodedString
 
+NON_TYPE_NAMES = {'pointer', 'const'}

comment_data.body:
> I think it'd be better to be more selective about loading the view submodule only when view is specifically looked. I don't know how hard that is though.

The memoryview in CythonScope is needed only when `cython.view` is imported/used?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@
 from . import MemoryView
 from .StringEncoding import EncodedString
 
+NON_TYPE_NAMES = {'pointer', 'const'}

@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@
 from . import MemoryView
 from .StringEncoding import EncodedString
 
+NON_TYPE_NAMES = {'pointer', 'const'}

comment_data.body:
> I think it'd be better to be more selective about loading the view submodule only when view is specifically looked. I don't know how hard that is though.

I think it makes sense. For now, I expanded the list but I will try to investigate whether we can avoid loading MemoryView in case `cython.view` is not used/imported.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@
 from . import MemoryView
 from .StringEncoding import EncodedString
 
+NON_TYPE_NAMES = {'pointer', 'const'}

comment_data.body:
I agree with @da-woods that this is really a stopgap solution. We should keep looking for a better way to initialise and integrate only what is truly needed.

In the meantime, the change here (hopefully) allows us to run tests with the shared module which is dearly needed.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@
 from . import MemoryView
 from .StringEncoding import EncodedString
 
+NON_TYPE_NAMES = {'pointer', 'const'}

commit.message: Do not initialize Cython scope when cython non-type attribute is used
